feedback_signal: Two fresh QA Disproof agents produced packet-bound blocked_validation records because the five-minute artifact-probe freshness window elapsed before their final evidence artifact could be written, even though the second agent observed all required local validations passing.
root_operational_failure: The child packet treated a short-lived artifact-writability probe as the freshness authority for an entire QA run. That makes normal instruction loading, independent analysis, and result preservation race a five-minute clock, and it repeatedly converts otherwise useful QA evidence into stale control artifacts.

durable_improvement.action: Define artifact-probe freshness as a dispatch-time writability check and bind result freshness to the child attestation timestamp plus immutable target SHA; require a deterministic fallback runner after two probe-expiry failures instead of another agent retry.
durable_improvement.prevents: Independent QA does not repeatedly fail because its artifact probe expires before normal bounded validation and final evidence write complete.

commands.command: PYTHONPATH=/Users/jamiecraik/dev/jamie-brain uv run --project /Users/jamiecraik/dev/jamie-brain python -c '<validate both CC1 QA packet/result bindings>'
commands.evidence: Both QA blocked_validation results are packet-bound, non-empty, and preserved; they identify the same probe-expiry failure rather than a source defect.

proof.future_behavior: After two expired QA probes at one immutable target, the OC stops spawning replacement agents and uses the governed deterministic QA fallback with separately recorded non-independence and artifact freshness.
